name: Chaturaji
author: Abstract Games factory
description: Chaturaji — Chaturanga for four players (medieval India, ~10th c.), the four-handed dice-chess reconstructed by Cox and Forbes and recorded by Murray. Four corner armies of King, Rook/Elephant, Knight/Horse, Boat (a 2-square diagonal leaper) and four Pawns move clockwise: Red, Green, Yellow, Black. Two long dice (2-5) pick which arm may move (2=Boat, 3=Knight, 4=Rook, 5=King/Pawn) for up to two moves a turn. No check or checkmate — kings are captured like any man and you score by the value of the pieces you take (King 5, Rook 4, Knight 3, Boat 2, Pawn 1); four boats in a 2x2 square capture the other three. Take all three enemy kings with your own alive to sweep the board. A diceless free-move variant is available.
